data.title: B2Gold's Q2 2025 results: Higher gold production and lower cash costs, strong operating performance.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, all three operations demonstrated strong operational performance, resulting in gold production surpassing expectations and cash costs falling below projections. This performance was reflected in higher-than-anticipated gold production levels and lower-than-expected cash costs for the period. The positive outcomes were driven by the continued strong operational efficiency across all three operations during this quarter.

data.title: Canacol Energy Ltd. reports $13.9M net income for Q2 2025. Financial results for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025.
data.description: Canacol Energy Ltd. (TSX:CNE; OTCQX:CNNEF; BVC:CNEC) based in Calgary, Alberta, announced its financial and operating performance for the quarter and half-year ending on June 30, 2025. All figures are in United States dollars, except for Canadian dollar unit prices specified as “C$”.

data.title: Colabor Group Inc. updates cybersecurity incident on Aug. 7, 2025, following July 21st announcement, working with experts.
data.description: In Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, on August 7, 2025, Colabor Group Inc. (TSX: CGL) issued an update on a cybersecurity breach reported on July 21st. The breach had affected Colabor's internal IT systems. Following the discovery of the incident, the company engaged top external cybersecurity professionals to manage and examine the breach.

data.title: Alliant Energy Corp. (NASDAQ: LNT) reports Q2 2025 earnings per share (EPS): GAAP EPS - $0.74, Non-GAAP EPS - $0.74.
data.description: In Madison, Wisconsin, Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ: LNT) revealed their unaudited earnings per share (EPS) for the three months ending on June 30. Their generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) consolidated EPS for 2025 was $0.74, a significant increase from $0.33 in 2024. Meanwhile, their non-GAAP consolidated EPS for the same period showed strong growth, with 2025 at $0.74 compared to $0.56 in 2024. Additionally, American Transmission Company (ATC) Holdings reported consistent EPS of $0.04 for both years. The results showed substantial improvements in Utilities and Corporate Services division.

data.title: At least 67 infected, 3 deaths in Central Harlem Legionnaires' outbreak prompts safety concerns.
data.description: An outbreak of Legionnaires' disease in Central Harlem has resulted in the illness of a minimum of 67 individuals and caused the deaths of three people. This has sparked worrying questions about the safety of buildings and the supervision of water systems in the area. The New York City Health Department has reported...

data.title: Atlassian exceeds Q2 estimates with $1.38 billion revenue, $928 million in cloud revenue. Partners with Google Cloud AI.
data.description: Atlassian Corp shares fell in after-hours trading following the announcement of financial results and a partnership with Alphabet Inc. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $1.38 billion, a 22% increase year-over-year, surpassing the consensus estimate of $1.36 billion. Earnings per share were 98 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 86 cents. Subscription revenue for the quarter was $1.31 billion, up 23% year-over-year, with cloud revenue reaching $928 million, a 26% increase year-over-year. Atlassian's full-year revenue reached $5.2 billion, marking a 20% increase year-over-year.

data.title: Ouster Inc's Q2 revenue: $35.05 million, beats estimates. Stock surges after reporting losses at 38 cents/share.
data.description: Ouster Inc (NASDAQ: OUST) released its Q2 financial results exceeding expectations after market close. The lidar company reported a revenue of $35.05 million, surpassing estimates of $33.77 million. Loss per share was 38 cents, lower than the estimated 55 cents. OUST saw significant gains in the latest session, with a 30% year-over-year and 7% sequential increase in total revenue. They shipped 5,500 sensors in Q2. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.description: Roman Storm's journey is far from ordinary, starting with teaching himself how to code and eventually landing odd jobs in the United States upon immigrating.

data.title: Firm Capital Mortgage Investment Corp reports Q2/2025 results, declares monthly dividends for October and November.
data.description: Today, in Toronto on August 7, 2025, the Firm Capital Mortgage Investment Corporation (referred to as the “Corporation”) disclosed its financial records for the first half of the year concluding on June 30, 2025. The Corporation’s financial statements are available for public scrutiny and trading under various stock symbols including TSX FC, FC.DB.J, FC.DB.K, and FC.DB.L.

data.title: Eli Lilly's obesity pill Orforglipron shows 12% weight loss in Phase 3 trial with 3,000 obese patients, trails Novo Nordisk.
data.description: Eli Lilly and Co. saw a significant drop in stock value after releasing Phase 3 trial results for orforglipron, an investigational GLP-1 receptor agonist targeting obesity in over 3,000 patients without diabetes. All doses of orforglipron met primary and secondary endpoints at 72 weeks, with the highest dose leading to an average weight loss of 12.4% (27.3 lbs) compared to 0.9% (2.2 lbs) with placebo. This weight reduction exceeded Wall Street's 15% expectation, with 59.6% of high-dose participants losing at least 10% body weight, and 39.6% losing at least 15%. Comparatively, Novo Nordisk A/S' oral semaglutide 25 mg achieved a 16.6% weight loss. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Serve Robotics stock falls as Q2 earnings miss expectations: $0.24 loss per share vs. expected $0.21, revenue of $642,000.
data.description: After the market closed on Thursday, Serve Robotics, Inc. (NASDAQ: SERV) revealed its second-quarter financial results. The report indicated a quarterly loss of 24 cents per share, falling short of the estimated 21 cents loss. However, the company surpassed revenue expectations with $642,000 in quarterly revenue compared to the projected $624,800. For real-time updates on SERV stock performance, refer to the live stock price and chart. Visit the Benzinga.com website for further details on this announcement.

data.title: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. prices private offering of $650 million in Senior Notes at 6.750% interest due 2034.
data.description: PennyMac Financial Services, Inc. and its subsidiaries have disclosed the pricing details of their recent offering, totaling $650 million in aggregate principal amount of 6.750% Senior Notes due 2034. The Notes, bearing interest at 6.750% per annum, are set to mature on February 15, 2034. Interest payments on the Notes will be made semi-annually on February 15 and August 15 of each year, with the first payment scheduled to begin on an upcoming date.
